sources.relevantConclusion: Current documentation recommends .agents/skills for repository Skills and describes metadata-first discovery.
A Plugin adds a manifest, packaged skills and marketplace installation; it is unnecessary for the current single-repository compatibility target.
Trusted projects may load project-scoped .codex/config.toml; permissions and skills.config can constrain future runtime workers.
Custom agents require name, description and developer instructions and may include model, sandbox, MCP and Skill settings.
Codex owns subagent orchestration; the FrontierScan YAML registry alone is not an active runtime.

decision: Keep .codex/skills for codex-cli 0.144.1 because direct target-runtime evidence passed; re-evaluate on CLI upgrade or additional Codex surfaces.
